commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: For whosoever shall call upon the name of the Lord shall be saved. God himself, when he was seen by Moses, said to him: “My name is the Lord. ” This is the Son of God, who is said to be both a messenger and God. The Son is not to be confused with the Father from whom all things come, but is to be acknowledged as the One through whom all things come and to whom all things belong. He is called God because the Father and the Son are one. He is also called an angel, because he was sent by the Father to announce the promised salvation. Commentary on Paul’s Epistles.

commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: By faith in Jesus Christ is granted to us both possession of the little beginning of salvation and its perfecting, which we await in hope.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: The words “Everyone who believes” point out faith, but the words “Everyone who calls upon” point out confession.
